title: More Nonsense Limerick 84
content:
There was an old man in a tree,
    Whose whiskers were lovely to see;
    But the birds of the air 
    Pluck'd them perfectly bare,
    To make themselves nests in that tree.
meaning: About: Edward Lear was an English artist, illustrator and writer known for his literary nonsense, in poetry and prose, and especially his limericks, a form which he popularised.
